description: A domestic cat is 'misheko', whether it sleeps on a household bed or keeps its distance from every household in sight. Temperament and human relationship belong to the particular animal's story. Wild cat species keep their own names.
articulatory_notes: The lips meet for nasal 'm' while voice passes through the nose, then open into high-front 'i'. The tongue blade rises for stressed unvoiced 'she'; a closure at the soft palate releases final 'ko'.
usage_notes: Optional 'lipha' can appear in a count. 'keloe' (companion) before 'misheko' makes that relation explicit; the bare animal noun makes no such claim.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The entry begins with the animal. Companion or unwelcome visitor, its relation to a household has to be said and can therefore be answered.

semantic_domains.community: a cat participating in or near human households
semantic_domains.nature: the domestic cat as an animal
